Gentiana saxosa, also known as alpine gentian, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and improving cognitive function. The bioactive constituents of Gentiana saxosa include gentianine, gentiopicroside, and swertiamarin, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Gentiana saxosa is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and improve sleep quality.
